title: PM Awas Yojana (PMAY)
meta_description: PM Awas Yojana 2026 — get up to ₹2.67 lakh subsidy on home loan. Check eligibility by income, apply online, track application status, and understand PMAY-Urban vs PMAY-Gramin.

hero.one_liner: Government subsidy of up to ₹2.67 lakh on home loans to make housing affordable for economically weaker sections and middle-income families

what_is_it: Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana (PMAY) is the government's flagship housing scheme launched on 25 June 2015 with the mission of 'Housing for All'. The scheme provides interest subsidy on home loans, making it cheaper for economically weaker sections (EWS), low-income groups (LIG), and middle-income groups (MIG) to buy or build their first home.

PMAY has two components: PMAY-Urban (for urban and semi-urban areas) and PMAY-Gramin (for rural areas). Under the urban component, eligible beneficiaries get an interest subsidy of 3% to 6.5% on home loan EMIs through the Credit Linked Subsidy Scheme (CLSS). Under the rural component, beneficiaries get direct financial assistance of up to ₹1.20 lakh (plains) or ₹1.30 lakh (hilly/difficult areas) to build a house.

Since launch, PMAY has sanctioned over 1.2 crore houses under the urban component and over 2.9 crore houses under the rural component, making it one of the largest housing programs globally. The interest subsidy alone saves beneficiaries ₹2.35–2.67 lakh over the loan tenure, making home ownership affordable for families earning ₹3–18 lakh annually.

The scheme has been extended multiple times and the current phase runs through 2024-2026. The government launched PMAY 2.0 in September 2024 with revised guidelines and additional coverage for urban poor.

eligibility.label: Who can apply
eligibility.value: Indian citizens who do not own a pucca house in their own or any family member's name anywhere in India

eligibility.label: EWS Category
eligibility.value: Annual household income up to ₹3 lakh — gets 6.5% interest subsidy on loans up to ₹6 lakh

eligibility.label: LIG Category
eligibility.value: Annual household income ₹3–6 lakh — gets 6.5% interest subsidy on loans up to ₹6 lakh

eligibility.label: MIG-I Category
eligibility.value: Annual household income ₹6–12 lakh — gets 4% interest subsidy on loans up to ₹9 lakh

eligibility.label: MIG-II Category
eligibility.value: Annual household income ₹12–18 lakh — gets 3% interest subsidy on loans up to ₹12 lakh

eligibility.label: Property requirement
eligibility.value: Must be a first-time home buyer — no pucca house in family's name in any part of India

eligibility.label: Documents needed
eligibility.value: Aadhaar card, income proof (salary slips or ITR), address proof, property documents, bank loan sanction letter, passport-size photos

extra_sections.heading: How Much Subsidy Will You Get? — Exact Calculations
How the Interest Subsidy Works — Example
content: Let's say you're in the LIG category (income ₹4 lakh/year) and take a home loan of ₹10 lakh for 20 years at 8.5% interest from a bank.
Without PMAY: Your EMI would be approximately ₹8,678/month. Total repayment over 20 years = ₹20.83 lakh.

With PMAY: The government gives you 6.5% interest subsidy on the first ₹6 lakh of your loan. This subsidy is calculated as a lump sum (Net Present Value) and credited to your loan account upfront, effectively reducing your outstanding loan amount. The subsidy amount works out to approximately ₹2.67 lakh.

After subsidy: Your effective loan reduces to about ₹7.33 lakh. Your new EMI drops to approximately ₹6,358/month. You save about ₹2,320 every month for 20 years — a total saving of ₹2.67 lakh over the loan tenure.

Important: The subsidy is only on the first ₹6/₹9/₹12 lakh of the loan (depending on your category). If your loan is larger, you pay normal interest on the remaining amount. But the subsidy portion gives you significant relief on EMIs.

extra_sections.heading: PMAY-Urban vs PMAY-Gramin
rows: Direct cash assistance to build house
Subsidy credited to loan account, reduces EMI
Money transferred in 3 installments to bank account
Minimum 25 sq.m. with kitchen, toilet, and LPG connection
pmaymis.gov.in or through bank/housing finance company
Self-selection by applying with income proof
Beneficiaries selected from SECC 2011 data by Gram Sabha

extra_sections.heading: Common Rejection Reasons and How to Avoid Them
content: Rejection: 'Applicant already owns a pucca house' — PMAY checks property records across India. If you, your spouse, or any dependent family member owns a pucca house anywhere, you're ineligible. Joint ownership also counts. Make sure to declare honestly — fraudulent claims result in legal action and subsidy recovery.

Rejection: 'Income exceeds category limit' — Your household income (not just your individual income) must be within the category limits. Household income includes all earning members. If your combined family income exceeds ₹18 lakh, you don't qualify for any PMAY category. Income is verified through salary slips, ITR, and bank statements.

Rejection: 'Property size exceeds limit' — Each category has a maximum carpet area limit (30 sq.m. for EWS, 60 for LIG, 160 for MIG-I, 200 for MIG-II). If your house exceeds this, the subsidy application will be rejected. Check the carpet area (not super built-up area) in your property documents.

Rejection: 'Aadhaar not linked to application' — Aadhaar is mandatory for PMAY. Both the applicant and co-applicant's Aadhaar must be linked. If your Aadhaar details don't match your bank/loan documents, the application gets stuck. Ensure name spelling matches exactly.

Rejection: 'Already availed PMAY benefit' — PMAY benefit can only be taken once per family. If any family member has already received PMAY subsidy, the family is permanently ineligible for another PMAY benefit.

how_to_apply.title: Check which category you fall in
how_to_apply.description: Calculate your annual household income (all earning members combined). EWS: up to ₹3L, LIG: ₹3-6L, MIG-I: ₹6-12L, MIG-II: ₹12-18L. Your category determines the subsidy amount and eligible loan size.

how_to_apply.title: Apply online or through your bank
how_to_apply.description: For PMAY-Urban: Visit pmaymis.gov.in → click 'Citizen Assessment' → select 'Under CLSS' → fill the application form with Aadhaar, income details, and property information. OR apply through your home loan bank — most banks (SBI, HDFC, ICICI, LIC Housing) have PMAY desks and can process your subsidy application along with the home loan.

how_to_apply.title: Submit income and property documents
how_to_apply.description: Provide: Aadhaar card, income certificate or ITR for last 2 years, salary slips, property allotment letter or sale agreement, approved building plan (for construction), and bank loan sanction letter.

how_to_apply.title: Bank verifies and forwards to NHB/HUDCO
how_to_apply.description: Your lending bank verifies your eligibility and forwards the subsidy claim to the Central Nodal Agency (NHB for MIG, HUDCO for EWS/LIG). This verification takes 2-4 weeks.

how_to_apply.title: Subsidy gets credited to your loan account
how_to_apply.description: Once approved, the subsidy amount (₹2.30-2.67 lakh) is transferred as a lump sum to your home loan account. Your outstanding loan reduces, and your EMI is recalculated at the lower amount. You start paying the reduced EMI from the next month.

how_to_apply.title: Track your application
how_to_apply.description: Track status at pmaymis.gov.in → 'Track Your Assessment Status'. Enter your Aadhaar number or application ID. You can also check with your lending bank's PMAY desk.

important_alert.text: PMAY benefit is ONE-TIME per family. You or your family members should not already own a pucca house or have availed PMAY earlier. Fraudulent claims result in subsidy recovery + legal action. Helpline: 1800-11-3388 (toll-free).

important_dates.label: PMAY-Urban CLSS
important_dates.value: Active — apply through bank or pmaymis.gov.in

faqs.question: Can I get PMAY subsidy if I already have a home loan?
faqs.answer: Yes! If you took a home loan after June 2015 and haven't availed PMAY yet, you can still apply for the subsidy retroactively. Apply through your bank — the subsidy will be credited to your existing loan account, reducing your outstanding principal.

faqs.question: Is PMAY only for first-time home buyers?
faqs.answer: Yes. You and your family members (spouse + unmarried children) should not own a pucca house anywhere in India. This is verified through property records and self-declaration. If you own a plot without a house, you may still be eligible for the construction component.

faqs.question: Can women apply for PMAY independently?
faqs.answer: Yes, and it's encouraged. For EWS and LIG categories, the house must be in the name of a female family member or co-owned with a female member. This is a mandatory requirement, not optional. For MIG categories, there is no such mandatory female ownership requirement.

faqs.question: What is the difference between carpet area and built-up area?
faqs.answer: Carpet area is the actual usable floor area inside the walls. Built-up area includes walls and balconies. PMAY limits are based on carpet area, which is typically 20-30% less than built-up area. A house with 160 sq.m. built-up area has roughly 120-128 sq.m. carpet area. Always check carpet area in your agreement.

faqs.question: Can I use PMAY for house renovation?
faqs.answer: No. PMAY-Urban CLSS is only for purchasing or constructing a new house. It cannot be used for renovation, repair, or extension of an existing house. PMAY-Gramin does support new construction including houses that replace existing kutcha houses.

faqs.question: How long does the subsidy take to get credited?
faqs.answer: Typically 2-6 months from the date of application. The process involves: bank verification (2-4 weeks), forwarding to NHB/HUDCO (1-2 weeks), NHB/HUDCO approval (2-4 weeks), and fund transfer to your bank (1-2 weeks). Track status at pmaymis.gov.in.

faqs.question: What if my income increases after getting PMAY?
faqs.answer: PMAY eligibility is based on your income at the time of application. If your income increases later, the subsidy is not revoked. However, if it's discovered that you misrepresented your income at the time of application, the subsidy will be recovered with interest.

faqs.question: Can I get PMAY if I'm self-employed?
faqs.answer: Yes. Self-employed individuals are eligible. You need to provide income proof — last 2 years' ITR, bank statements, and a self-declaration of income. A CA certificate may also be required for higher income categories.

ministry: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs
